title: allof/anyof use .all()/.any() short-circuit iterators correctly
description: eval_allof and eval_anyof (evaluator.rs:243-255) use Iterator::all() and Iterator::any() which short-circuit on the first false/true result. A reviewer might suggest replacing with explicit loops for 'clarity.' The iterator form is more idiomatic Rust and the short-circuit property is implicit in the standard library guarantee for all/any, so it is both correct and idiomatic. The single-line implementation is the right level of abstraction here.
